factors.summary: Organic growth is mixed, with evidence of success in backlog increases from new business awards and acquisitions, but a deceleration in organic revenue growth despite contributions from recent acquisitions.

evidence.text: The increase in backlog in Critical Mission Solutions (CMS) from April 2, 2021 was primarily driven by success in closing on a number of key opportunities in the U.S government space and the BlackLynx acquisition. The increase in backlog in People & Places Solutions (P&PS) from April 2, 2021 was primarily driven by new business awards in our advanced facilities business and the StreetLight acquisition.

For the six months ended April 1, 2022, revenues were $7.21 billion, an increase of $285.0 million, or 4.1%, from $6.93 billion for the corresponding period last year. Revenue increases for the year over year periods were due mainly to fiscal 2022 incremental revenues from the PA Consulting investment and the StreetLight, BlackLynx and Buffalo group acquisitions, as well as benefits from increased spending in our U.S. government business sector client base.

factors.summary: The company's debt leverage is mixed. While there is no indication of covenant breaches and net interest expense has increased due to higher average debt from recent acquisitions, there is no specific information on changes to debt levels or refinancing activities.

evidence.text: We were in compliance with all of our debt covenants at April 1, 2022.

Net interest expense for the three and six months ended April 1, 2022 was $21.6 million and $39.5 million, respectively, an increase of $6.8 million and $8.5 million from $14.9 million and $31.0 million for the corresponding periods last year. The increase in net interest expense for the three and six month periods year over year is due to higher levels of average debt outstanding relating in part to the funding of the PA Consulting investment as well as the StreetLight and BlackLynx acquisitions, in addition to higher interest rates.

evidence.text: Operating margins are improving due to higher operating profit levels, driven by acquisitions.

Gross profit for the second quarter of 2022 was $870.4 million, an increase of $103.4 million, or 13.5%, from $767.0 million from the corresponding period last year. Our gross profit margins were 22.7% and 21.6% for the three months ended April 1, 2022 and April 2, 2021, respectively, with these margin differences being mainly attributable to favorable margin impacts from the business results of our recent PA Consulting investment and the StreetLight, BlackLynx and Buffalo Group acquisitions, as well as benefits from increased spending by customers in the U.S. government business sector.
